[{"role": "system", "content": "# TASK
1. Analyze the provided labeled examples to identify the observable visual features that distinguish each class.
2. Examine the Query Image and compare it strictly against the provided examples.
3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Query Image.

# INSTRUCTIONS
- Base your decision only on observable visual evidence in the examples and the Query Image.
- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.
- Use only labels from the provided final options list.
- Choose exactly one final label.
- Output only the requested XML tags and not any aditional ones.
- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.
- Follow the condition-specific XML blocks first, then output exactly one final <response> block.
- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>final_class</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

First list the critical observable features present only in the Query Image.
Then formulate a knowledge base (kb) with a small set of IF-THEN rules grounded in observable patterns from the labeled examples in the support images and the Query Image, for all classes. Try to unify the conditions so that there is only one rule per class.
Rules must refer only to visible features.
Then check which rule(s) are best satisfied by the Query Image.

<features>
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
</features>
<kb>
- IF [observable visual features = feature value AND/OR observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class1]
- IF [observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class2]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- State which rule(s) from the kb fires for the Query Image and briefly explain why,
referring only to the features listed above. Do not discuss other rules or introduce new features.
</rule_check>

<response>final_class</response>

The content of <response> must be exactly one label copied verbatim from the provided options list."}, {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "What is the class of this image?"}, {"type": "image_url", "url_kind": "data_uri", "length": 10907}]}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<response>81</response>"}, {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "What is the class of this image?"}, {"type": "image_url", "url_kind": "data_uri", "length": 15155}]}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<response>14</response>"}, {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Query Image:
Classify this image using only the labeled examples above.
Valid labels: [81, 14]
Follow the exact XML structure required by the system instructions."}, {"type": "image_url", "url_kind": "data_uri", "length": 10235}]}]

raw_response_text:
<features>
- observable feature: flower has multiple large petals with a distinct star-like shape
- observable feature: petals are light pink with darker pink or purple veining near the center
- observable feature: center of flower has a dense cluster of stamens
</features>
<kb>
- IF [flower has multiple large petals with a distinct star-like shape AND petals are light pink with darker veining near center AND center has dense stamens] THEN [81]
- IF [flower has three upright petals (standards) and three drooping petals (falls) AND petals are yellow AND flower has a prominent upright petal (dorsal sepal)] THEN [14]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- Rule for class 81 fires because the Query Image shows a flower with multiple large petals forming a star-like shape, light pink petals with darker veining near the center, and a dense cluster of stamens in the center, which matches the features described in the rule for class 81.
</rule_check>
<response>81</response>

original_messages.content:
# TASK
1. Analyze the provided labeled examples to identify the observable visual features that distinguish each class.
2. Examine the Query Image and compare it strictly against the provided examples.
3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Query Image.

# INSTRUCTIONS
- Base your decision only on observable visual evidence in the examples and the Query Image.
- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.
- Use only labels from the provided final options list.
- Choose exactly one final label.
- Output only the requested XML tags and not any aditional ones.
- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.
- Follow the condition-specific XML blocks first, then output exactly one final <response> block.
- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>final_class</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

First list the critical observable features present only in the Query Image.
Then formulate a knowledge base (kb) with a small set of IF-THEN rules grounded in observable patterns from the labeled examples in the support images and the Query Image, for all classes. Try to unify the conditions so that there is only one rule per class.
Rules must refer only to visible features.
Then check which rule(s) are best satisfied by the Query Image.

<features>
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
</features>
<kb>
- IF [observable visual features = feature value AND/OR observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class1]
- IF [observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class2]
</kb>

<rule_check>
- State which rule(s) from the kb fires for the Query Image and briefly explain why,
referring only to the features listed above. Do not discuss other rules or introduce new features.
</rule_check>

<response>final_class</response>

The content of <response> must be exactly one label copied verbatim from the provided options list.

content.text: What is the class of this image?
What is the class of this image?

Query Image:
Classify this image using only the labeled examples above.
Valid labels: [81, 14]
Follow the exact XML structure required by the system instructions.

raw_response_text:
<features>
- observable feature: flower has three upright petals (standards) and three drooping petals (falls)
- observable feature: petals are bright yellow with darker yellow veins
- observable feature: flower has a distinct shape with a prominent central fall
</features>
<kb>
- IF [flower has three upright petals and three drooping petals AND petals are bright yellow with darker veins] THEN [14]
- IF [flower has multiple pink petals with a star-like shape and a fuzzy center] THEN [81]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- Rule 1 fires because the Query Image shows a yellow flower with three upright and three drooping petals, matching the description for class 14.
</rule_check>
<response>14</response>
